message:
feat(core): migrate commit repair to asupersync runtime with Cx propagation

Replace thread::spawn with asupersync runtime task spawning in
CommitRepairCoordinator, adding proper Cx capability context propagation
through repair workers. This enables structured cancellation and
cooperative task lifecycle management.

Key changes:
- CommitRepairCoordinator now accepts Runtime + parent Cx, spawning
  repair tasks via runtime.handle().try_spawn() instead of thread::spawn
- Add wall-clock Instant tracking (recorded_at) to CommitRepairEvent for
  real latency measurement vs logical event ordinals
- Wrap repair task bodies in AssertUnwindSafe/catch_unwind so a panicking
  generator marks the task Failed without aborting other in-flight repairs
- Extract repair logic into standalone async RepairTask struct
- GroupCommitCoordinator::run_loop now takes &Cx instead of &AtomicBool,
  using cx.checkpoint() / cx.is_cancel_requested() for shutdown
- Add WAL offset count validation in group commit to fail fast on
  append_batch returning mismatched offset count
- Fix commit_seq pairing in group commit responses to use positional zip
  instead of find-by-txn_id (correct for duplicate txn_ids in same batch)
- Connection: add WriteCoordinator region + task registration/spawning
  infrastructure with independent child Cx per task
- Harness: update CommitRepairHarness constructor for new Runtime + Cx
  parameters and add pending_background_repair_count assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
